name: South Ossetia (Georgia)

colors.symbolism: wealth, abundance, and the prosperity of the people

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of white (top), red (middle), and yellow (bottom).
history: Adopted on 26 November 1990 by the Supreme Council of the South Ossetian Autonomous Oblast and reaffirmed on 30 March 1992 after the unilateral declaration of independence. South Ossetia is internationally recognised as a region of Georgia (ISO code GE-SO) but has been administered as a de-facto independent state since the early 1990s; the flag is used in this de-facto capacity. It is identical in colours and arrangement to the flag of North Ossetia–Alania (a federal subject of Russia, RU-SE).
